_meta.description: Per-element safety and strategic metadata for the disorder-screening pipeline.
_meta.toxicity_filter_threshold: Elements with is_radioactive=true or is_toxic=true are removed by stage4_viability when constraints.non_toxic and constraints.non_radioactive are enabled.

sources.toxicity: IARC Monographs, NIOSH IDLH database, RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU, REACH SVHC list
sources.eu_crm: EU Critical Raw Materials Act 2023 (Annex I & II)

_meta.cost_annotation: Order-of-magnitude USD/kg for bulk oxide; annotation only, not used for filtering.

Al.toxicity_notes: Safe; widely used in food packaging and pharmaceuticals.

As.toxicity_notes: IARC Group 1 carcinogen (inorganic arsenic). RoHS restricted. Severely restricts any commercial cathode application.

B.toxicity_notes: Low acute toxicity. Boric acid is a mild irritant at high doses.

Bi.toxicity_notes: Low toxicity; used medically (Pepto-Bismol). Less toxic than As or Sb.

Co.toxicity_notes: IARC 2B (Co metal/sulfate dust). In oxide lattice at low concentration, risk is minimal. Host element in NMC.

Cr.toxicity_notes: Cr3+ itself is benign, but NMC synthesis involves 700-900°C calcination in O2-rich atmosphere. Cr6+ (hexavalent chromium, chromates/dichromates) is an IARC Group 1 carcinogen and RoHS Annex II restricted substance. Oxidation of Cr3+ to Cr6+ under synthesis conditions is well-documented.

Cu.toxicity_notes: Essential micronutrient. Toxic only at extremely high doses. No concern in battery context.

Fe.toxicity_notes: Essential element. Safe in all battery contexts.

Ga.toxicity_notes: Low acute toxicity. Some concern for Ga compounds at very high doses, but safe at dopant concentrations.

Ge.toxicity_notes: Low toxicity in oxide form. GeH4 is toxic but not relevant here.

Hf.toxicity_notes: Low toxicity; similar chemistry to Zr which is biocompatible.

Ir.toxicity_notes: PGM; low acute toxicity in elemental and oxide forms. IrO2 is used in electrodes.

Mn.toxicity_notes: Essential element. Chronic inhalation of Mn dust (manganism) is a concern in manufacturing, but at oxide dopant concentrations the risk is minimal. Already present in NMC host.

Mo.toxicity_notes: Low acute toxicity. Essential trace element. MoO3 dust is a mild irritant.

Nb.toxicity_notes: Low toxicity; biocompatible (used in surgical implants).

Ni.toxicity_notes: Ni metal is IARC 2B; some Ni compounds (NiSO4) are IARC Group 1 carcinogens. In oxide lattice at dopant concentrations, risk profile is low and consistent with NMC811 standard handling. REACH SVHC for soluble Ni compounds.

Os.toxicity_notes: Osmium tetroxide (OsO4) forms spontaneously when Os-containing materials are heated in air. OsO4 is one of the most acutely toxic volatile compounds: TLV = 0.0002 mg/m3, IDLH = 0.001 ppm. NMC synthesis at 700-900°C in O2 atmosphere makes Os use extremely hazardous in any production setting.

Pt.toxicity_notes: PGM; Pt metal is inert and safe. Some Pt complexes are allergens but Pt oxide is benign.

Re.toxicity_notes: Low toxicity; rhenium compounds have low acute hazard.

Rh.toxicity_notes: PGM; low toxicity in elemental and oxide forms.

Ru.toxicity_notes: PGM; RuO4 can form at high temperature (volatile, mildly toxic) but less severe than OsO4. At typical synthesis temperatures, risk is manageable with standard fume hoods.

S.toxicity_notes: Elemental sulfur is non-toxic. SO2 fumes during synthesis need ventilation but this is standard practice. Note: S5+ is a highly unusual oxidation state at a TM octahedral site.

Sb.toxicity_notes: IARC 2B possible carcinogen (antimony trioxide). RoHS Directive restricted substance. Sb compounds have moderate acute and chronic toxicity. Also confirmed_failed in NMC ground truth (phase separation above ~2%).

Sc.toxicity_notes: Low toxicity; limited experimental data but no significant hazard identified.

Se.toxicity_notes: Essential trace element but toxic at higher doses (selenosis). Environmental persistence concern. Not filtered by default — annotated for experimentalist awareness.

Sn.toxicity_notes: SnO2 is used in food containers. Low toxicity in oxide form.

Ta.toxicity_notes: Biocompatible; used in surgical implants. Very low toxicity.

Ti.toxicity_notes: Biocompatible; used in medical implants and food colouring (TiO2).

U.toxicity_notes: Alpha-emitting radioactive element (U-238 t1/2 = 4.5 Gyr; U-235 is fissile). Also chemically toxic (heavy metal nephrotoxin). Regulated as nuclear material in all jurisdictions. Completely incompatible with any commercial battery application.

V.toxicity_notes: V2O5 dust has NIOSH IDLH of 35 mg/m3; concern during powder handling. In sintered oxide lattice, risk is minimal. Widely used in vanadium redox flow batteries.

W.toxicity_notes: Low toxicity; used in light bulb filaments and medical imaging. WO3 is essentially non-toxic.

Y.toxicity_notes: Low toxicity; used in phosphors and ceramics.

Zr.toxicity_notes: Biocompatible; used in dental ceramics and implants. ZrO2 is essentially inert.
